item.title: 模型的 Django 管理工具。 | Django Admin Tools for a model.
item.description:
维杰·索尼

发表于在本教程中，您将了解 django admin 中模型的一些内置工具以及如何应用它们。

假设我们有一个模型Article 。

 # models.py from django.db import models class Article ( models . Model ): title = models . CharField ( max_length = 250 ) slug = models . SlugField ( max_length = 250 ) author = models . ForeignKey ( User , on_delete = models . CASCADE , related_name = 'articles' ) body = models . TextField () published_on = models . DateTimeField ( auto_now_add = True ) def __str__ ( self ): return self . title

我们希望在我们的管理站点中为这个模型提供以下功能。

  1. 我们不想只显示模型title字段的字符串表示。 我们还想在模型条目上表示author和published_on 。

  2. 我们想创建过滤器来对模型的条目进行排序。

  3. 我们希望在文章条目页面上进行搜索以找到所需的条目。

  4. 我们想在title字段的基础上预填充模型的slug字段。

  5. 我们想以所需的格式订购模型的条目。

为了做到这一点，我们有 django 的简单方法🦄。

所以让我们开始吧！

为此，我们必须进入admin.py文件并为我们的Article模型创建一个ModelAdmin并设置一个list_display列表。

 # admin.py file from django.contrib import admin from .models import Article class ArticleAdmin ( admin . ModelAdmin ): # displaying title, author and publish date. list_display = [ 'title' , 'author' , 'published_on' ] admin . site . register ( Article , ArticleAdmin )

现在它将用模型的title代表author和published_on的_on。

为此，我们必须使用list_filter ArticleAdmin

 # admin.py file from django.contrib import admin from .models import Article class ArticleAdmin ( admin . ModelAdmin ): # displaying title, author and publish date. list_display = [ 'title' , 'author' , 'published_on' ] # creating filter to sort the entries by publish date. list_filter = [ 'published_on' ,] admin . site . register ( Article , ArticleAdmin )

您将在右侧看到一个过滤器，您可以使用它按发布日期过滤模型条目。

您还可以创建过滤器以按作者对文章进行排序，但这不是一个好方法，因为如果用户数量增加，过滤器将变得比所有条目长。

要创建搜索栏，我们只需在“ArticleAdmin”类中添加“search_fields”。

 # admin.py file from django.contrib import admin from .models import Article class ArticleAdmin ( admin . ModelAdmin ): # displaying title, author and publish date. list_display = [ 'title' , 'author' , 'published_on' ] # creating filter to sort the entries by publish date. list_filter = [ 'published_on' ,] # creating the search bar. search_fields = [ 'title' , 'body' ] admin . site . register ( Article , ArticleAdmin )

在search_fields列表中添加要搜索的字段，您将看到页面顶部出现一个搜索栏。
您不应在此foreignkey列表中添加search_fields键字段，因为它不支持它。

我们希望在title字段的基础上自动创建slug 。 为此，我们必须在ArticleAdmin prepopulated_fields字典。

 # admin.py file from django.contrib import admin from .models import Article class ArticleAdmin ( admin . ModelAdmin ): # displaying title, author and publish date. list_display = [ 'title' , 'author' , 'published_on' ] # creating filter to sort the entries by publish date. list_filter = [ 'published_on' ,] # creating the search bar. search_fields = [ 'title' , 'body' ] # prepopulating slug in the basis of title. prepopulated_fields = { 'slug' : ( 'title' ,)} admin . site . register ( Article , ArticleAdmin )

现在 slug 字段将由title字段自动生成。

为此，我们只需在ArticleAdmin的ordering列表中添加我们想要排序的字段。

 # admin.py file from django.contrib import admin from .models import Article class ArticleAdmin ( admin . ModelAdmin ): # displaying title, author and publish date. list_display = [ 'title' , 'author' , 'published_on' ] # creating filter to sort the entries by publish date. list_filter = [ 'published_on' ,] # creating the search bar. search_fields = [ 'title' , 'body' ] # prepopulating slug in the basis of title. prepopulated_fields = { 'slug' : ( 'title' ,)} # ordering the model by the publish date. ordering = [ 'published_on' ,] admin . site . register ( Article , ArticleAdmin )

现在您将看到按发布日期订购文章的选项。

额外提示💡

如果我们想通过一些操作或计算显示模型的任何字段，我们可以在模型中创建一个专用的方法，然后在ModelAdmin的list_display中调用方法名称。

例如，如果我们想显示Article body的前 50 个字符，我们可以这样做：

 # models.py from django.db import models class Article ( models . Model ): title = models . CharField ( max_length = 250 ) slug = models . SlugField ( max_length = 250 ) author = models . ForeignKey ( User , on_delete = models . CASCADE , related_name = 'articles' ) body = models . TextField () published_on = models . DateTimeField ( auto_now_add = True ) def __str__ ( self ): return self . title def content ( self ): ''' represents the first 50 characters of the body field.''' return f " { self . body [ : 50 ] } ..."
 # admin.py file from django.contrib import admin from .models import Article class ArticleAdmin ( admin . ModelAdmin ): # displaying title, author and publish date. list_display = [ 'title' , 'content' , 'author' , 'published_on' ] # creating filter to sort the entries by publish date. list_filter = [ 'published_on' ,] # creating the search bar. search_fields = [ 'title' , 'body' ] # prepopulating slug in the basis of title. prepopulated_fields = { 'slug' : ( 'title' ,)} # ordering the model by the publish date. ordering = [ 'published_on' ,] admin . site . register ( Article , ArticleAdmin )

完成了。

感谢您阅读我的文章。

一旦暂停，vijaysoni007 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，vijaysoni007 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，vijaysoni007 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 vijaysoni007 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 vijaysoni007 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 vijaysoni007 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Vijay Soni

Posted on Jul 30

In this tutorial you will learn about some built in tools for a model in django admin and how to apply them.

Suppose we have a model Article.

# models.py

from django.db import models class Article(models.Model): title = models.CharField(max_length=250) slug = models.SlugField(max_length=250) author = models.ForeignKey(User, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='articles') body = models.TextField() published_on = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True) def __str__(self): return self.title

and we want following functionalities in our admin site for this model.

  1. We don't want to show only the string representation of the title field of the model. We also want to represent author and published_on on the model entry.

  2. We would like to create filters to sort the entries of the model.

  3. We would like to have a search on the article entries page to find the desired entry.

  4. We would like to prepopulate the slug field of the model in the basis of title field.

  5. We would like to order the entries of the model in desired format.

to do so we have simple ways by grace of django 🦄.

So let's get started!

1. Representing many fields of a model in the admin site.

to do this we have to go in the admin.py file and create a ModelAdmin for our Article model and set a list_display list.

# admin.py file
from django.contrib import admin
from .models import Article class ArticleAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): # displaying title, author and publish date.
 list_display = ['title', 'author', 'published_on'] admin.site.register(Article, ArticleAdmin)

now it will represent both author and published_on with the title of the model.

2. Creating filters to sort the model entries.

to do this we have to update our ArticleAdmin with list_filter.

# admin.py file
from django.contrib import admin
from .models import Article class ArticleAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): # displaying title, author and publish date.
 list_display = ['title', 'author', 'published_on'] # creating filter to sort the entries by publish date.
 list_filter = ['published_on',] admin.site.register(Article, ArticleAdmin)

You will see a filter has occurred on the right side which you can use to filter the model entries by publish date.

You can also create a filter to sort the articles by the author but it's not a good approach because if the number of users increases the filter will become longer than the all entries.

3. Creating the search bar.

To create a search bar we just have to add 'search_fields' in our 'ArticleAdmin' class.

# admin.py file
from django.contrib import admin
from .models import Article class ArticleAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): # displaying title, author and publish date.
 list_display = ['title', 'author', 'published_on'] # creating filter to sort the entries by publish date.
 list_filter = ['published_on',] # creating the search bar.
 search_fields = ['title', 'body'] admin.site.register(Article, ArticleAdmin)

add the fields you want to search with in the search_fields list and you will see a search bar occurs on the top of the page.
You should not add a foreignkey field in this search_fields list because it does not support it.

4. Prepopulating slug in the basis of title.

We would like to get the slug automatically created in the basis of the title field. To do this we have to create a dictionary named prepopulated_fields in our ArticleAdmin.

# admin.py file
from django.contrib import admin
from .models import Article class ArticleAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): # displaying title, author and publish date.
 list_display = ['title', 'author', 'published_on'] # creating filter to sort the entries by publish date.
 list_filter = ['published_on',] # creating the search bar.
 search_fields = ['title', 'body'] # prepopulating slug in the basis of title.
 prepopulated_fields = {'slug': ('title',)} admin.site.register(Article, ArticleAdmin)

now the slug field will be automatically generated by the title field.

5. Ordering the article entries by the publish date.

to do this we just have to add the fields which we want to sort in the ordering list of the ArticleAdmin.

# admin.py file
from django.contrib import admin
from .models import Article class ArticleAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): # displaying title, author and publish date.
 list_display = ['title', 'author', 'published_on'] # creating filter to sort the entries by publish date.
 list_filter = ['published_on',] # creating the search bar.
 search_fields = ['title', 'body'] # prepopulating slug in the basis of title.
 prepopulated_fields = {'slug': ('title',)} # ordering the model by the publish date.
 ordering = ['published_on',] admin.site.register(Article, ArticleAdmin)

now you will see the option to order the articles by the publish date.

Extra tip 💡

If we want to show any field of the model with some operation or calculation we can create a dedicated method in the model and then call the method name in the list_display of the ModelAdmin.

for example if we want to show the first 50 characters of the body of the Article we could do it like this:

# models.py

from django.db import models class Article(models.Model): title = models.CharField(max_length=250) slug = models.SlugField(max_length=250) author = models.ForeignKey(User, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='articles') body = models.TextField() published_on = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True) def __str__(self): return self.title def content(self): ''' represents the first 50 characters of the body field.''' return f"{self.body[:50]} ..."

# admin.py file
from django.contrib import admin
from .models import Article class ArticleAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin): # displaying title, author and publish date.
 list_display = ['title', 'content', 'author', 'published_on'] # creating filter to sort the entries by publish date.
 list_filter = ['published_on',] # creating the search bar.
 search_fields = ['title', 'body'] # prepopulating slug in the basis of title.
 prepopulated_fields = {'slug': ('title',)} # ordering the model by the publish date.
 ordering = ['published_on',] admin.site.register(Article, ArticleAdmin)

that's done.

Thank you for reading my article.

Once suspended, vijaysoni007 will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, vijaysoni007 will be able to comment and publish posts again.

item.title: React JS 中的路由 | Routings in React JS
item.description:
神秘客

发表于大家好，今天我将向您展示如何使用 react-router-dom 路由到网站中的不同页面。

它支持在 React 应用程序中各种组件的视图之间导航，允许更改浏览器 URL，并使 UI 与 URL 保持同步。

首先在您的终端中输入这些命令-

 npm install --save react-router-dom reactstrap bootstrap

然后我们将导入所需的模块-

 import React ,{ useState } from ' react ' import { Navbar , NavbarBrand , Nav , NavItem , NavLink , Collapse , NavbarToggler } from ' reactstrap ' ; //importing react-strap components import { BrowserRouter as Router , Link , Route , Routes } from ' react-router-dom ' ; //importing routing elements

首先，我们将为 HOME、ABOUT 和 CONTACT 页面创建三个函数，用于路由。

 / Home page function function Home () { return < h1 className = " text-center display-3 text-primary " > This is Home Page < /h1 > } //About page function function About () { return < h1 className = " text-center display-3 text-success " > This is About Page < /h1 > } //Contact page function function Contact () { return < h1 className = " text-center display-3 text-danger " > This is Contact Page < /h1 > }

然后我们将使用路由器组件作为导航栏的入口点。

 < Router > //your navbar < /Router >

然后我们将使用 react-strap 创建一个导航栏。

 < Navbar dark color = " faded " className = " text-dark " style = {{ backgroundColor : " #1F2833 " }} > < NavbarBrand style = {{ color : " peachpuff " , fontSize : " 3rem " }} > Coder Academy < /NavbarBrand > < NavbarToggler onClick = { isToggle } / > < Collapse isOpen = { toggle } navbar > < Nav navbar className = " text-center " > < NavItem > < NavLink style = { Links } > < Link to = " / " > Home < /Link > < /NavLink > < /NavItem > < NavItem > < NavLink style = { Links } > < Link to = " /about " > About < /Link > < /NavLink > < /NavItem > < NavItem > < NavLink style = { Links } > < Link to = " /contact " > Contact < /Link > < /NavLink > < /NavItem > < /Nav > < /Collapse > < /Navbar >

您是否注意到我们使用了链接标签来包装我们的链接
好吧，它是一个反应路由器组件，它指向当您单击该链接时链接将采用的路径。

您可以像这样使用 Link 标签。

此处的“to”属性用于指向链接将带您到达的 url。

接下来，我们将使用 Switch 标签来提供当我们使用我们的链接路由到某个路径时要看到的组件。

在这里，您可以如何使用 Routes 标签。

 < Routes > < Route exact path = " / " element = { < Home /> } /> < Route path = " /about " element = { < About /> } / > < Route path = " /contact " element = { < Contact /> } / > < /Routes >
  • 这里的标签用于路由到 url 附加到的路径。 因此，当用户点击 Home 链接时，Route 将加载 Home 函数中的内容。 同样，当用户单击 About 链接时，Route 将加载 About 功能组件内的内容，对于 Contact 链接也是如此。
  • 在这里，'exact' 属性用于匹配确切的 url，如果没有找到则转到下一个。

  • 这是完整的代码 -

     import React ,{ useState } from ' react ' import { Navbar , NavbarBrand , Nav , NavItem , NavLink , Collapse , NavbarToggler } from ' reactstrap ' ; //importing react-strap components import { BrowserRouter as Router , Link , Route , Switch } from ' react-router-dom ' ; //importing routing elements //Styling the Links const Links = { color : " palevioletred " , fontSize : " 2.5rem " , margin : " 2rem 0 " , fontWeight : " 600 " , } //Home page function function Home () { return < h1 className = " text-center display-3 text-primary " > This is Home Page < /h1 > } //About page function function About () { return < h1 className = " text-center display-3 text-success " > This is About Page < /h1 > } //Contact page function function Contact () { return < h1 className = " text-center display-3 text-danger " > This is Contact Page < /h1 > } function ReactStrap () { const [ toggle , setToggle ] = useState ( false ); const isToggle = () => setToggle ( ! toggle ); return ( < div > < Router > < Navbar dark color = " faded " className = " text-dark " style = {{ backgroundColor : " #1F2833 " }} > < NavbarBrand style = {{ color : " peachpuff " , fontSize : " 3rem " }} > Coder Academy < /NavbarBrand > < NavbarToggler onClick = { isToggle } / > < Collapse isOpen = { toggle } navbar > < Nav navbar className = " text-center " > < NavItem > < NavLink style = { Links } > < Link to = " / " > Home < /Link > < /NavLink > < /NavItem > < NavItem > < NavLink style = { Links } > < Link to = " /about " > About < /Link > < /NavLink > < /NavItem > < NavItem > < NavLink style = { Links } > < Link to = " /contact " > Contact < /Link > < /NavLink > < /NavItem > < /Nav > < /Collapse > < /Navbar > < Routes > < Route exact path = " / " element = { < Home /> } /> < Route path = " /about " element = { < About /> } / > < Route path = " /contact " element = { < Contact /> } / > < /Routes > < /Router > < /div > ) } export default ReactStrap

    谢谢你查看这篇文章^^你可以通过下面的链接捐款帮助我谢谢👇👇 ^^

    还要检查这些帖子
    一旦暂停，shubhamtiwari909 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，shubhamtiwari909 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，shubhamtiwari909 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 shubhamtiwari909 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 shubhamtiwari909 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 shubhamtiwari909 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Mysterio

Posted on Jul 30

Hello Everyone today i am going to show you how you can route to different page in a website using react-router-dom.

React Router is a standard library for routing in React. It enables the navigation among views of various components in a React Application, allows changing the browser URL, and keeps the UI in sync with the URL.

Firstly enter these commands in your terminal-

npm install --save react-router-dom reactstrap bootstrap

Then we are going to import the required modules-

import React,{useState} from 'react'
import {Navbar,NavbarBrand,Nav,NavItem,NavLink,Collapse,NavbarToggler} from 'reactstrap';//importing react-strap components
import {BrowserRouter as Router,Link,Route,Routes} from 'react-router-dom';//importing routing elements

First we will create three function for HOME,ABOUT and CONTACT pages which we will use to routing.

/Home page function
function Home() { return <h1 className="text-center display-3 text-primary">This is Home Page</h1>
} //About page function
function About() { return <h1 className="text-center display-3 text-success">This is About Page</h1>
} //Contact page function
function Contact() { return <h1 className="text-center display-3 text-danger">This is Contact Page</h1>
}

Then we will use Router component as our entry point to the Navigation bar.

<Router>
//your navbar
</Router>

Then we will create a navbar using react-strap.

 <Navbar dark color="faded" className="text-dark" style={{backgroundColor:"#1F2833"}}> <NavbarBrand style={{color:"peachpuff",fontSize:"3rem"}}> Coder Academy </NavbarBrand>
 <NavbarToggler onClick={isToggle} />
 <Collapse isOpen={toggle} navbar> <Nav navbar className="text-center"> <NavItem> <NavLink style={Links}> <Link to="/">Home</Link>
 </NavLink>
 </NavItem>
<NavItem> <NavLink style={Links}> <Link to="/about">About</Link>
 </NavLink>
 </NavItem>
<NavItem> <NavLink style={Links}> <Link to="/contact">Contact</Link>
 </NavLink>
 </NavItem>
 </Nav>
 </Collapse>
 </Navbar>

Did you notice that we have used Link tags to wrap our links
Well it is a react router component which points to the path the link will take when you click on that link.

You can use the Link tag like this.

Here the 'to' attribute is used to point to the url which the link will take you to.

Next we will use the Switch tag to provide the components to be seen when we route to some path using our link.

Here how you can use the Routes tag.

 <Routes> <Route exact path="/" element={<Home />} />   <Route path="/about" element={<About />} />
 <Route path="/contact" element={<Contact />} />
 </Routes>
  • Here tag is used to route to the path the url is attached to. So, when the user clicks on Home link then Route will load the content inside the Home function. Similarly when the user clicks on About link then Route will load the content inside the About function component and same for the Contact link.
  • Here the 'exact' attribute is used to match the exact url then goes to the next one if not found.

  • 'path' attribute is used to map the url to the component which needs to be rendered when we route to that url. (It means when we click the Home link then the contents inside Home component will be rendered).

  • 'element' attribute is used to pass the element which will be rendered when the url is matched to the Route

Here is the full code -


import React,{useState} from 'react'
import {Navbar,NavbarBrand,Nav,NavItem,NavLink,Collapse,NavbarToggler} from 'reactstrap';//importing react-strap components
import {BrowserRouter as Router,Link,Route,Switch} from 'react-router-dom';//importing routing elements //Styling the Links
const Links = { color:"palevioletred", fontSize:"2.5rem", margin:"2rem 0", fontWeight:"600",
} //Home page function
function Home() { return <h1 className="text-center display-3 text-primary">This is Home Page</h1>
} //About page function
function About() { return <h1 className="text-center display-3 text-success">This is About Page</h1>
} //Contact page function
function Contact() { return <h1 className="text-center display-3 text-danger">This is Contact Page</h1>
} function ReactStrap() { const [toggle, setToggle] = useState(false); const isToggle = () => setToggle(!toggle); return ( <div> <Router> <Navbar dark color="faded" className="text-dark" style={{backgroundColor:"#1F2833"}}> <NavbarBrand style={{color:"peachpuff",fontSize:"3rem"}}> Coder Academy </NavbarBrand>
 <NavbarToggler onClick={isToggle} />
 <Collapse isOpen={toggle} navbar> <Nav navbar className="text-center"> <NavItem> <NavLink style={Links}> <Link to="/">Home</Link>
 </NavLink>
 </NavItem>

<NavItem> <NavLink style={Links}> <Link to="/about">About</Link>
 </NavLink>
 </NavItem>
 <NavItem> <NavLink style={Links}> <Link to="/contact">Contact</Link>
 </NavLink>
 </NavItem>
 </Nav>
 </Collapse>
 </Navbar>
 <Routes> <Route exact path="/" element={<Home />} />   <Route path="/about" element={<About />} />
 <Route path="/contact" element={<Contact />} />
 </Routes>

</Router>
 </div>
 )
} export default ReactStrap

item.title: 如何自学 CS | How to Self-Teach CS
item.description:
古斯塔夫·布罗曼

发表于有两种类型的程序员：

  1. 那些了解幕后发生的事情的人。
  2. 那些因为熟悉一些高级工具而勉强过关的人。

Don't know about you, but I do not want to be a code monkey.

如果你也想进化，你必须自学 CS。

我总结了每个实践软件工程师都应该知道的 9 个基本 CS 主题：

一旦暂停，gdbroman 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，gdbroman 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，gdbroman 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

item.title: 代理模式 trong Javascript | Proxy Pattern trong Javascript
item.description:
参数 thứ 2 của proxy là một object đại diện được gọi là handler 。 代理确保 tôi không sủa đổi object person với các value không hợp lệ, điều này giúp tôi giữ cho dữ liệu của tôi tinh khiết（数据纯）
反映
方法 trên Reflect có cùng tên với các 方法 trên object đang xử lý。
代理 là một cách hiệu quả để 控制 các 行为 của một 对象。 马蒂亚斯·安德拉施—— 一旦暂停，dylanvo28 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，dylanvo28 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，dylanvo28 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 dylanvo28 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 dylanvo28 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 dylanvo28 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Với một Proxy object, chúng ta có quyền kiểm soát các tương tác với một object nhất định, ví dụ: getting một value hoặc setting một value.

Hãy tạo một object person, đại diện là John Doe.

const person = {
  name: "John Doe",
  age: 42,
  nationality: "American"
};

Thay vì tương tác trực tiếp với object này, tôi muốn tương tác với một proxy object. Trong javascript, tôi có thể tạo một proxy bằng cách sử dụng từ khóa new Proxy

const person = {
  name: "John Doe",
  age: 42,
  nationality: "American"
};

const personProxy = new Proxy(person, {});

Argument thứ 2 của proxy là một object đại diện được gọi là handler. Trong handler object, tôi có thể xác định các hành vi cụ thể dựa trên loại tương tác. Mặc dù có nhiều methods mà tôi có thể thêm vào Proxy handler, 2 method phổ biến nhất là get và set:

  • get: được gọi khi cố gắng access một property
  • set được gọi khi muốn modify một property Quy trình sẽ xảy ra như xau: Thay vì tương tác trực tiếp với object person, tôi sẽ tương tác với personProxy. Hãy thêm các handlers vào proxyHandler. Khi muốn modify một property, do đó gọi method set trên proxy, tôi muốn proxy in value cũ và value mới, sau đó thực hiện ghi đè value của property. Khi cố gắng access một property, hãy gọi method get trên Proxy, tôi muốn proxy ghi lại một contains chứa key và value của property. 
const personProxy = new Proxy(person, {
  get: (obj, prop) => {
    console.log(`The value of ${prop} is ${obj[prop]}`);
  },
  set: (obj, prop, value) => {
    console.log(`Changed ${prop} from ${obj[prop]} to ${value}`);
    obj[prop] = value;
  }
});

Tuyệt hãy xem đoạn code dưới dây, điều gì sẽ xảy ra?

const person = {
  name: "John Doe",
  age: 42,
  nationality: "American"
};

const personProxy = new Proxy(person, {
  get: (obj, prop) => {
    console.log(`The value of ${prop} is ${obj[prop]}`);
  },
  set: (obj, prop, value) => {
    console.log(`Changed ${prop} from ${obj[prop]} to ${value}`);
    obj[prop] = value;
    return true;
  }
});

personProxy.name; //The value of name is John Doe 
personProxy.age = 43; //Changed age from 42 to 43

Khi access name, proxy trả về một câu: The value of name is John Doe
khi modify age, proxy in Changed age from 42 to 43

Ngoài ra có thể bắt các validation của method proxy

const personProxy = new Proxy(person, {
  get: (obj, prop) => {
    if (!obj[prop]) {
      console.log(
        `Hmm.. this property doesn't seem to exist on the target object`
      );
    } else {
      console.log(`The value of ${prop} is ${obj[prop]}`);
    }
  },
  set: (obj, prop, value) => {
    if (prop === "age" && typeof value !== "number") {
      console.log(`Sorry, you can only pass numeric values for age.`);
    } else if (prop === "name" && value.length < 2) {
      console.log(`You need to provide a valid name.`);
    } else {
      console.log(`Changed ${prop} from ${obj[prop]} to ${value}.`);
      obj[prop] = value;
    }
  }
});

Hãy xem đoạn code dưới dây, điều gì sẽ xảy ra?

const person = {
  name: "John Doe",
  age: 42,
  nationality: "American"
};

const personProxy = new Proxy(person, {
  get: (obj, prop) => {
    if (!obj[prop]) {
      console.log(`Hmm.. this property doesn't seem to exist`);
    } else {
      console.log(`The value of ${prop} is ${obj[prop]}`);
    }
  },
  set: (obj, prop, value) => {
    if (prop === "age" && typeof value !== "number") {
      console.log(`Sorry, you can only pass numeric values for age.`);
    } else if (prop === "name" && value.length < 2) {
      console.log(`You need to provide a valid name.`);
    } else {
      console.log(`Changed ${prop} from ${obj[prop]} to ${value}.`);
      obj[prop] = value;
    }
    return true;
  }
});

personProxy.nonExistentProperty; //Hmm.. this property doesn't seem to exist
personProxy.age = "44"; //Sorry, you can only pass numeric values for age. 
personProxy.name = ""; //You need to provide a valid name.

Proxy made sure tôi không sủa đổi object person với các value không hợp lệ, điều này giúp tôi giữ cho dữ liệu của tôi tinh khiết(data pure)
Reflect
Js cung cấp một object gọi là Reflect, giúp tôi thao tác với đối tượng đích dễ dàng hơn khi làm việc với proxy.
Trước đây, tôi muốn modify và acces property trên một object bên trong proxy thông qua get, set bằng ky hiệu {}. Thay vào đó, tôi có thể sử dụng Reflect. Methods trên Reflect có cùng tên với các methods trên object đang xử lý.
Thay vì access property thông qua obj[prop], tôi có thể access hoặc modify property thông qua Reflect.get() và Reflect.set(). Các methods nhận các arguments dưới dạng methods trên handle obejct.

const personProxy = new Proxy(person, {
  get: (obj, prop) => {
    console.log(`The value of ${prop} is ${Reflect.get(obj, prop)}`);
  },
  set: (obj, prop, value) => {
    console.log(`Changed ${prop} from ${obj[prop]} to ${value}`);
    Reflect.set(obj, prop, value);
  }
});

Hãy xem đoạn code dưới dây:

const person = {
  name: "John Doe",
  age: 42,
  nationality: "American"
};

const personProxy = new Proxy(person, {
  get: (obj, prop) => {
    console.log(`The value of ${prop} is ${Reflect.get(obj, prop)}`);
  },
  set: (obj, prop, value) => {
    console.log(`Changed ${prop} from ${obj[prop]} to ${value}`);
    return Reflect.set(obj, prop, value);
  }
});

personProxy.name; //The value of name is John Doe 
personProxy.age = 43; //The value of name is John Doe 
personProxy.name = "Jane Doe"; //The value of name is John Doe

Proxy là một cách hiệu quả để control các behavior của một object. Một proxy có thể có nhiều use-cases: nó giúp validation, formatting, notifications, hoặc debugging.
Việc lạm dụng proxy hoặc thực hiện các thao tác nặng trên mỗi handler method có thể ảnh hưởng đến performance của app. Tốt nhất cũng không nên sử dụng proxy cho các đoạn code quan trọng về hiệu suất.

Matthias Andrasch - Jul 21

Once unsuspended, dylanvo28 will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by dylanvo28 will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

item.title: 与 Postman 的 API 101 会话 | API 101 Session with Postman
item.description:
罗尼特·卡特里

发表于所以今天早上，我参加了由 Postman 学生领导团队 API101 主持的精彩会议。 有机会了解很多关于邮递员社区、公共工作区、如何使用 GET、POST、PUT 和 DELETE 进行收藏的知识。 在本次会议中，我们首先了解了什么是 API。 本次会议主要关注 API 的基础知识，为什么要使用它，它是如何工作的，以及我们如何使用 Postman 正确使用它。 我以前不知道几乎所有东西都使用 API。 首先，会话从发出请求开始，这就是我们与 API 交互的方式。 从这里我们得到了三个部分，方法地址/端点和路径。 之后，我了解了诸如 200 OK,404 Not Found 之类的状态代码。 然后，我们在 Postman 上进行了实践，并在其中导航到 Postman Student API 101 Workshop。 然后他们帮助我们理解基本的 Auth、Robust API 和变量。 在所有这些理论之后，我终于做了一些有趣的实践。 我使用 GET、POST、PUT 和 DELETE 等 API 操作创建了自己的 API。

毕竟这是与邮递员社区的一次很棒的会议。

什么是 API？ 为什么现在这么有必要？

  • 动手实验室经验
  • 身份验证和东西

    • 因此，让我与您分享我从会议中学到的东西，让您对 API 感到满意。

    什么是 API？

    那么，让我们举一个 API 是如何工作的例子

    假设您，客户，访问您最喜欢的餐厅并要求您最喜欢的比萨饼。 您不会去厨房直接为您的比萨点厨师，而是告诉服务员进一步与厨师沟通。
    所以在这里服务员充当了你和厨师之间的一种沟通方式。 以类似的方式，API 的作用是充当软件之间通信的中间人。

    上面的描述向我们展示了在软件开发世界中，客户端（需要数据的人）如何将 API 接受的请求发送到服务器（存储数据的位置）并相应地执行操作。

    为什么需要 API？ 为什么不直接使用来自服务器的数据？

    列昂尼德·维戈夫斯基—— 暂停后，ronit02_65 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    解除暂停后，ronit02_65 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，由 ronit02_65 发布的所有帖子都将被隐藏，并且只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 ronit02_65 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 ronit02_65 的方法：

    举报其他不当行为

    取消标记 ronit02_65 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Ronit Khatri

    Posted on Jul 30

    So today in the morning, I attended an amazing session conducted by Postman Student leader team, API101. Got a chance to learn a lot about the postman community, public workspace, how to make a collection using GET,POST,PUT and DELETE. In this session we first learnt about What are API's. This session mainly focused on the basics of API why should one use it, how it works, and how we can properly use it using Postman. I previously didn't knew API is used in almost everything. Postman has literally changed how the world used to see API using the curl command line. Postman has simplified it with its UI. First the session started with making requests that is how we interact with the API. From this we got three parts the methods address/endpoints and the Path. After that I got to know about the Status codes such as 200 OK,404 Not Found. We then had the hands on practice on Postman in which we navigated to the Postman Student API 101 Workshop. Then they help us to understand basic Auth, Robust API's and variables. After All this theory I finally got to do some interesting practical. I made my own API using API operations like GET,POST,PUT and DELETE.

    Afterall it was a great session with the Postman Community.

    ..#PostmanStudent #PostmanAPI

    1. What's an API? Why is it so necessary nowadays?
    2. How does Postman solve the problem!!!
    3. Hands on lab experience
    4. Authentication and stuff

    So let me share with you my learnings from the session and make you comfortable with API's.

    What's an API?

    API: stands for Application Programming Interface
    So, lets take an example of how does an API work

    Suppose you, the customer, visit your favorite restaurant and asks for your favorite pizza. You won't go to the kitchen and directly order the chef for your pizza but instead you tell the waiter who further communicates with the chef.
    So here the waiter acts as a mode of communication between you and the chef. In the similar fashion, the role of an API is to act as the middleman for the communication between software's.

    The above depiction shows us how in the Software Development World, the client (the person requiring the data), sends a request which is taken by the API to the Server (where the data is stored) and the action is performed accordingly.

    Why is necessary to API? Why not directly use data from the servers?

    Leonid Vygovskiy - Jul 29

item.title: 考虑数据冗余解决方案作为医院计算机在热浪期间崩溃！ | Considering Data Redundancy Solutions as Hospital Computers Meltdown During Heatwave!
item.description:
在这一生中，我们认为有很多事情是理所当然的，其中包括我们医院的可靠性，当然还有他们的 IT 系统！ 在西方，尤其是在英国首都伦敦，我们已经习惯了我们医院提供的快速、可靠、准确和周到的服务。 特别是来自著名的盖伊和圣托马斯医院，这是一个卓越中心，也是英国领先的医院和社区医疗保健、研究和教育提供商之一。

想象一下我当时的震惊和沮丧，当我在周一带亲人去看门诊时，却被告知我们那天不会收到体检结果，因为医院的计算机系统自周五起就停机了！ 不是过去的 10 或 30 分钟、小时或一天，这已经够糟糕了，而是整个周末！

不过，我们是一些幸运的人，因为我们只是在等待常规测试结果。 通常非常称职的医院工作人员现在因无法访问或阅读任何患者的医疗记录而感到无助和沮丧。 结果，医院陷入了彻底的混乱。 至少一名癌症患者，可能还有更多人几乎服用了错误的药物，这将产生危险甚至可能致命的结果：

有一次在半夜，一位护士叫醒我，试图给我服用我从未听说过的药物。

脑洞大开！

由于医院的 IT 服务器在高温下出现故障，许多其他不幸的患者不得不取消他们的手术和程序。 众所周知，此类操作和程序的等待名单正在攀升。 所以想象一下，不得不等待几个月甚至几年的手术，结果却因为高温而在最后一刻被取消，这种挫败感和对个人健康的灾难性影响！ 另外，如果这就是一家领先的医院的应对方式，那么其他资源较少的医院呢？

正如我在上一篇博文中所讨论的那样，一些看似无害的天气，能够摧毁城市最重要服务的整个 IT 系统，是全球变暖的明确迹象。 它还强调需要考虑更有效的数据和存储冗余解决方案。

在得知医院系统出现故障后，我的脑海中立刻浮现了我在学习和通过 Microsoft Azure 基础 AZ-900 考试时学到的云概念。 数据和存储冗余。

这些计算服务包括：数据库、存储、服务器、软件、网络、分析和智能。 云计算采用按需付费模式，以更低的成本提供更快、更可靠、更灵活、更灵活、更可扩展的服务。 可靠性是通过数据和/存储冗余来实现的。

那么什么是数据和/存储冗余，在这种情况下它有什么帮助呢？

让我们首先从冗余开始。 冗余是指复制您的系统并在稍微不同的位置拥有一个备份系统。 这样可以确保在您的主系统发生故障时，该备份系统可以启动，就像上面的 Guys and St Thomas' Hospital 中的 IT 服务器发生故障一样。

这种冗余的有效性取决于备份系统的地理位置。 冗余选项包括可用区和区域对。

下图显示了一个共享地理区域中的三个（最低要求）单独的可用区。 例如，在同一个城市或行政区的三个独立建筑物中。 每个可用区由一个或多个数据中心组成，配备独立的电源、冷却和网络。 这是为了确保在一个可用区（站点）发生断电、冷却和/或网络中断的事件时，其他两个可用区中的任何一个的备用系统都准备好接管。 这确保了最少的停电和停机时间。 破坏一个可用区的事件示例可能是本地网络上的病毒或闪电击中该可用区的建筑物。

每个可用区都设置为隔离边界，确保公共云上的其他客户/组织无法访问或破坏其他客户的数据。

可用区通过高速、专用和多样化的光纤网络连接。

对于患者记录等项目的存储，您有两个主要选项：本地冗余存储 (LRS) 和区域冗余存储 (ZRS)，如下图所示。

本地冗余存储 (LRS)：指的是您的数据在主区域的单个物理位置同步复制 3 次的解决方案。 自然，LRS 是最便宜的选择，因为它只需要一个物理位置。 但是，此选项不适用于需要高可用性或持久性的应用程序，例如患者记录。

区域冗余存储 (ZRS)：指的是在同一主要区域中的三个 Azure 可用区域之间同步复制数据的解决方案，如上图示例所示。

然而，如果灾难（例如上面现实生活中的热浪）影响到整个地区怎么办？ 这是区域对提供更大冗余的地方，这是 Microsoft 强烈推荐的，也是大多数组织采用的选项。 组织可以在不同的区域对中使用 LRS 和 ZRS 的组合。 因此，医院很可能也有一个区域对。 当然，前提是医院能够首先采用云技术。

区域对是每个 Azure 区域与同一地理区域内的另一个区域配对，但至少相距 300 英里。 如果区域对中的一个受到自然灾害的影响，那里的故障服务将自动故障转移到其区域对中的另一个区域，如下图所示。 区域对直接相连，但相距足够远，可以与区域性灾难隔离，例如热浪、洪水、风暴和恐怖袭击。 这确保了可靠的服务和数据冗余。

计划的 Azure 更新会一次推广到配对区域，以最大程度地减少停机时间并降低应用程序中断的风险。 除“巴西南部”外，数据继续位于同一地理边界内，符合税收和执法管辖权要求。

在区域对存储方面，Azure 提供了两种解决方案。 地理冗余存储 (GRS) 和地理区域冗余存储 (GZRS)。

异地冗余存储 (GRS)：这是使用 LRS 方法在主要区域内的单个物理位置同步复制数据三次的解决方案。 然后数据也被异步复制到次要区域内的单个物理位置。 在该次要区域内，您的数据也会使用 LRS 同步复制三次，就像在主要区域中一样。

地理区域冗余存储 (GZRS)：使用 ZRS 跨主要区域的三个 Azure 可用区域同步复制数据。 然后，您的数据会异步复制到次要区域中的单个物理位置。 在该次要区域内，您的数据随后会使用 LRS 同步复制三次。 在选择合适的云服务时，为了满足他们的需求，组织还需要在公共云、私有云或混合云之间进行选择。 私有云是组织购买和维护自己的硬件的地方，可以完全控制其资源和安全性。 如果医院采用了云服务，这可能是选择的模型，以避免与公共云相关的治理问题。

但是，私有云的缺点包括高资本成本和 TCO（总拥有成本），包括 IT 开销和浪费。 这是因为您正在为资源付费，这些资源很快就会过时并耗尽计算内存。 您也无法通过公共云尽可能快速高效地向外扩展、随着容量需求的增加和缩减而增加更多资源、在需求下降时卸载资源和相关成本。

一个解决方案可能是转向混合云以获得两全其美的优势，包括可管理的安全性和更大的停机恢复能力（因为计算维护是外包的，并使用最新的安全解决方案进行管理，例如 DDoS/Distributed-Denial-of-服务预防），但是这带来了它自己的兼容性问题和复杂性。

总而言之，解决方案是让 Guys 和 St Thomas' Hospital 等实体转向采用 Azure 等基于混合云的技术，如果他们还没有这样做的话。 在 Azure 数据和存储选项中，GZRS 解决方案具有最佳水平的 IT 服务可用性和持久性，将成为 Guys 和 St Thomas' Hospital 等客户的理想解决方案，对他们而言，对此类系统的依赖通常很重要生与死。 然而，这样的解决方案通常是最昂贵的，而且在公共部门削减的经济中，初级服务的预算正在被削减，通常会带来灾难性的后果。 此外，随着热浪变得更加全球化而不是区域性，这些解决方案也被推到了极限。

也许到那时，未来的解决方案不仅要考虑我们如何分配和备份电力、计算和网络服务，还要考虑（可再生）电力本身的替代形式，使用与地球温度变化而不是屈服的可持续技术给它。



原文:

There are many things we take for granted in this one life and that includes the reliability of our hospitals and of course their IT systems! In the West, especially in the UK’s capital city of London, we have become accustomed to a fast, reliable, accurate and attentive service from our hospitals. Especially from the renowned Guy’s and St Thomas’ Hospital, a centre of excellence, and one of the UK’s leading providers of hospital and community-based healthcare, research, and education.

Imagine my shock and dismay then, when taking a loved one to an outpatient appointment on the Monday, to be informed that we would not receive the results of the medical tests that day, due to the hospitals computer systems being down since Friday! Not the past 10 or 30 minutes, hour or day, which would have been bad enough, but over an entire weekend!

We were some of the lucky ones though, as we were only awaiting routine test results. Usually highly competent hospital staff, were now rendered helpless and frustrated at being unable to access or read any of their patients medical records. As a result the hospital fell into complete chaos. At least one cancer patient and possibly serveral more were almost given the wrong medication, which would have had hazardous and possibly fatal results:

_“On two occasions I was confused for being another patient. Once in the middle of the night a nurse woke me up to try and give me medication I had never heard of.

“After quite a long argument with this nurse it transpired she was standing with a different patient’s paper record.

“Another occasion a surgeon burst into my room to tell me he had been in my operation and that my appendix had ruptured.

“Again after another slightly long argument it transpired he had got the wrong person and was very apologetic.” — Jennifer, Cancer Patient, Guys and St Thomas’ Hospital, (Source: BBC News).
_

Mind Blown!

A copious number of other unlucky patients had to have their operations and procedures cancelled due to the hospitals IT servers failing in the heat. Its common knowledge that waiting lists for such operations and procedures are climbing. So imagine then the frustration, and disastrous impact on personal wellbeing of having to wait several months, possibly years, for an operation, only for it to be cancelled at the last minute, due to the heat! Plus if this is how one of the leading hospitals were coping, what then of other less resourced hospitals?

Something seemingly so innocuous as the weather, having the power to take down entire IT systems of the city’s most vital services, is a sure sign of global warming as discussed in my last blog post. It also emphasises the need to consider more effective data and storage redundancy solutions.

Upon learning of the hospitals failing systems, my mind instantly went to a cloud concept I had learnt while studying for and passing the Microsoft Azure Fundamentals AZ-900 Exam. That of both data and storage redundancy.

Azure, is Microsofts Cloud computing solution, and cloud computing refers to the delivery of computing services over the internet (the cloud). These computing services include: databases, storage, servers, software, networking, analytics, and intelligence. Cloud computing offers faster, and more reliable, agile, elastic and scalable services at lower cost due to its pay as you go model. Reliability is achieved through data and/ storage redundancy.

So what is data and/ storage redundancy and how could it have helped in this situation?

Let’s first start with redundancy. Redundancy refers to duplicating your system and having a backup system in a slightly different location. This ensures that this back-up system can kick into gear in the event that your primary system fails, just as the IT servers failed in the Guys and St Thomas’ Hospital, example above.

How effective this redundancy is depends on the geographical location of your back-up system. Redundancy options include Availability Zones and Region Pairs.

An Azure Availability Zone refers to having physically separate datacenters within the same Azure region, as illustrated by the diagram below. The diagram below displays three (the minimum required) separate availability zones, in one shared geographical region. So in three separate buildings in the same city or borough for example. Each availability zone comprises of one or more datacenters, equipped with an independent source of power, cooling and networking. This is to ensure that in the event of an incident knocking out the power, cooling and / or networking at one availability zone (site), then the back-up systems at either of the other two availability zones are ready to take over. This ensures minimum outtage and downtime. An example of an incident taking down one availability zone maybe a virus on a local network or lightning striking the building of that availability zone.

Each availability zone is set up to be an isolation boundary, ensuring that no other clients/organisations on the public cloud can access or compromise another clients data.

The availability zones are connected via high-speed, private and diverse fiber-optic networks.

In terms of storage for items such as patient records, you have two main options: Locally Redundant Storage (LRS) and Zone-redundant Storage(ZRS) as depicted in the illustration below.

Locally Redundant Storage(LRS): refers to the solution where your data is copied three times, synchronously within a single physical location in the primary region. Naturally, LRS is the least expensive option as it only requires a single physical location. However, this option is not suitable for applications requiring high availability or durability, such as patient records.

Zone-redundant Storage (ZRS): Refers to the solution where your data is copied synchronously across three Azure availabilty zones in the same primary region, as in the illustrated example above.

Yet what if disaster (such as a heatwave in the real life example above) affects the entire region? This is where a region pair provides even greater redundancy, is highly recommended by Microsoft, and an option, that most organisations adopt. Organisations can use a combination of LRS and ZRS in different region pairs. So it is highly likely that the hospital would have also had a region pair. That is of course if the hospital has been able to adopt cloud technology in the first instance.

A Region Pair is where each Azure region is paired with another region within the same geography, but at least 300 miles away. If one of the region pair is afflicted by a natural disaster, the failing services there would automatically failover to the other region in its region pair, as illustrated in the diagram below. The region pair are directly connected yet far enough apart to be isolated from regional disasters, such as heatwaves, floods, storms and terrorist attacks. This ensures reliable services and data redundancy.

Planned Azure updates are rolled out to paired regions, one region at a time, to minimize downtime, and mitigate risk of application outtage. With the exception of “Brazil South”, data continues to reside within the same geographic boundary, in-line with tax and law enforcement jurisdiction requirements.

In terms of region pair storage, Azure offers two solutions. Geo-redundant Storage(GRS) and Geo-zone redundant storage (GZRS).

Geo-redundant storage (GRS): This is the solution where your data is copied sychronously three times within a single physical location, within the primary region, using the LRS method. The data is then also copied asynchronously, to a single physical location within the secondary region. Within that secondary region your data is also copied three times sychronously, using LRS, just as in the primary region.

Geo-zone-redundant storage (GZRS): Is where your data is copied synchronously across three Azure availability zones in the primary region, employing ZRS. Your data is then copied asynchronously to a single physical location in the secondary region. Within that secondary region, your data is then copied synchronously three times employing LRS. The GZRS solution provides the highest availability and most durability as illustrated below:

When selecting the appropriate cloud service, for their needs, organisations also need to choose between the public, private or hybrid cloud. The private cloud, is where organisations purchase and maintain their own hardware, retaining complete control over their resources and security. If the hospital has adopted cloud services, this could be the model chosen, to avoid the governance issues associated with the public cloud.

However, drawbacks to the private cloud comprise of high capital costs and TCO (Total Cost of Ownership), including IT overheads, and wastage. This is because you are paying for resources, which become outdated and run out of compute memory very quickly. You also cannot scale out, adding more resources as demand for capacity increases and scale in, offloading resources and associated costs as demand falls, as quickly and efficiently as you can, with the public cloud.

A solution may be to move towards a hybrid cloud to obtain the best of both worlds, including manageable security and greater resilience to outtages (as compute maintainance is outsourced and managed using the latests security solutions, such as DDoS/Distributed-Denial-of-Service prevention), however this brings with it its own compatibility issues and complexities.

In conclusion, the solution would be for entities such as Guys and St Thomas’ Hospital, to move towards adopting Hybrid Cloud based technologies such as Azure, if they have not done so already. Within the Azure data and storage options, the GZRS solution, with its optimal levels of IT service availability and durability, would be the ideal solution for clients such as Guys and St Thomas’ Hospital, for whom the reliance on such systems is often a matter of life and death. However, such solutions are often the most expensive, and in an economy of public sector cuts, primary services are seeing their budgets slashed, often with catastrophic consequences. Plus with heat waves becoming more global rather than regional, such solutions are also being pushed to their limits.

Perhaps then, future solutions will also have to consider not only how we distribute and back-up power, compute and networking services, but also alternative forms of (renewable) power itself, using sustainable technologies which work with the earths changing temperatures rather than succumbing to it.
item.author: marshac713

item.title: 事件驱动的 API 文档变得简单（客户端渲染）。 | Event driven API documentation made simple (Client-Side Rendering).
item.description:
这篇文章与其他文章有点不同。

在我之前的帖子中，我为绝对初学者写过文章，但这篇文章是为希望为事件驱动项目自动化和形式化文档生成的开发人员编写的。

本指南面向寻找有关如何使用其 AsyncAPI 文件生成文档的说明的人们。 事件驱动 API 与您通常使用 OpenAPI 或 GraphQL 记录的同步 API 不同。 现在很多人都在使用 AsyncAPI，是时候为社区提供一份指南，说明社区在客户端呈现文档时需要哪些选项。

哦，如果您仍然不确定客户端渲染或服务器端渲染，那么您可以在这里阅读所有内容👉初学者网站渲染（无耻插件😉）。

我将介绍以下用途：

  • 所有示例都将使用相同的 AsyncAPI 示例文件👇

     { "asyncapi": "2.2.0", "info": { "title": "Account Service", "version": "1.0.0", "description": "This service is in charge of processing user signups" }, "channels": { "user/signedup": { "subscribe": { "message": { "$ref": "#/components/messages/UserSignedUp" } } } }, "components": { "messages": { "UserSignedUp": { "payload": { "type": "object", "properties": { "displayName": { "type": "string", "description": "Name of the user" }, "email": { "type": "string", "format": "email", "description": "Email of the user" } } } } } } }

    这是生成文档的预期外观👇

    本文中的所有使用示例都可用于检查asyncapi-docs-rendering-examples存储库。

    反应

    如果您希望在 React 应用程序中从您的 AsyncAPI 文件呈现文档，那么您需要使用AsyncAPI React 组件。

    在第4 行导入React AsyncAPI 组件。

    如果您对 AsyncAPI 样式感到满意，那么您需要使用import "@asyncapi/react-component/styles/default.min.css";

    第7 行是导入 AsyncAPI 示例文件的位置。

    如果您希望在 Vue 应用程序中从您的 AsyncAPI 文件生成文档，那么您需要使用AsyncApiStandalone bundle 。

    正如您在第 6 行看到的，您需要使用import AsyncApiStandalone from '@asyncapi/react-component/browser/standalone'; 命令。

    如果你喜欢 AsyncAPI 样式，那么你需要去👉 node_modules/@asyncapi/react-component/style/default.min.css 。

    复制该文件，然后将其粘贴到您的资产文件夹中。
    我重命名了我的 asyncapi.min.css 。

    您可以使用import './assets/asyncapi.min.css'在main.js文件中导入它，或者像我一样，将其添加到App.vue文件的末尾，并使用<style scope src="@/assets/asyncapi.min.css"></style> 。

    网页组件

    要从 AsyncAPI 文件生成文档，您可以将其用作 HTML 网页的元素或您选择的任何其他 Web 框架中的 Web 组件。 你可以通过实现web-react-components来做到这一点。

    这就对了！ 独立包

    如果您想在不使用任何框架但仅使用 HTML 网页的情况下从 AsyncAPI 文件呈现文档，那么您将需要Standalone bundle 。

    我差点忘了。

    还有另一种配置 AsyncAPI 组件的方法。
    你可以通过config props 来完成，这与普通的 React 组件相同。

    我的Web Component和Standalone Bundle使用示例有config='{"show": {"sidebar": false}}这会关闭侧边栏，但如果将其更改为true ，则呈现的文档将有一个侧边栏。

    这只是可以做的一个示例。 有关如何生成文档的更多信息，请查看AsyncAPI 文档。

    我希望你喜欢这个通过 AsyncAPI 客户端文档生成的快速指南。

    像往常一样，如果您发现任何错误，请不要在评论部分发疯。 只要让我知道，我们就可以一起修复它，这样这篇文章就会变得更好。



原文:

📢 Original article is available on my website where you can also find my Internship Journal.

This post is a bit different from the other articles.

In my previous posts, I have written for absolute beginners, but this one is for developers looking to automate and formalize documentation generation for event-driven projects.

This guide is directed toward folks looking for instructions on how to generate documentation using their AsyncAPI files. Event-driven APIs are not the same as the synchronous APIs you usually document with OpenAPI or GraphQL. Many people use AsyncAPI now, and it is time to provide the community with a guide that shows what options the community has to render documentation on the client-side.

Oh, if you are still not sure about Client-Side Rendering or Server-Side Rendering, then you can read all about it here 👉 Website rendering for beginners (shameless plug 😉).

I will cover the usage for:

  • React
  • Vue
  • Web Components
  • Standalone Bundle

All examples will use this same AsyncAPI sample file 👇

{
  "asyncapi": "2.2.0",
  "info": {
    "title": "Account Service",
    "version": "1.0.0",
    "description": "This service is in charge of processing user signups"
  },
  "channels": {
    "user/signedup": {
      "subscribe": {
        "message": {
          "$ref": "#/components/messages/UserSignedUp"
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "components": {
    "messages": {
      "UserSignedUp": {
        "payload": {
          "type": "object",
          "properties": {
            "displayName": {
              "type": "string",
              "description": "Name of the user"
            },
            "email": {
              "type": "string",
              "format": "email",
              "description": "Email of the user"
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

This is the expected look of the generated document 👇

All usage examples from this article are available to check on asyncapi-docs-rendering-examples repository.

React

If you wish to render documentation from your AsyncAPI file in React application, then you need to use AsyncAPI React component.

1️⃣ To install the React AsyncAPI component run the command npm install --save @asyncapi/react-component@next.

2️⃣ Now, create index.js file and type in:

import React from "react";
import ReactDOM from "react-dom";

import AsyncApiComponent from "@asyncapi/react-component";
import "@asyncapi/react-component/styles/default.min.css";

import { specMock } from "./testDoc";

const rootElement = document.getElementById("root");
ReactDOM.render(<AsyncApiComponent schema={specMock} />, rootElement);

React AsyncAPI component is imported on the line 4.

If you are happy with AsyncAPI styling then you need to import their CSS pattern with import "@asyncapi/react-component/styles/default.min.css";

On the line 7 is where AsyncAPI sample file is imported.

Vue

If you wish to generate documentation from your AsyncAPI file in Vue application, then you need to use AsyncApiStandalone bundle.

1️⃣ React AsyncAPI component is required here as well, so again you need to run npm install --save @asyncapi/react-component@next command.

2️⃣ In your App.vue just add this code:

<template>
  <div ref="asyncapi"></div>
</template>

<script>
import AsyncApiStandalone from '@asyncapi/react-component/browser/standalone';

// AsyncAPI specification, fetched or pasted.
const schema =
'{"asyncapi":"2.4.0","info":{"title":"Account Service","version":"1.0.0","description":"This service is in charge of processing user signups"},"channels":{"user/signedup":{"subscribe":{"message":{"$ref":"#/components/messages/UserSignedUp"}}}},"components":{"messages":{"UserSignedUp":{"payload":{"type":"object","properties":{"displayName":{"type":"string","description":"Name of the user"},"email":{"type":"string","format":"email","description":"Email of the user"}}}}}}}';

const config = {}; // Configuration for component. This same as for normal React component.

export default {
  name: 'AsyncApiComponent',
  props: {
    msg: String
  },
  mounted() {
    const container = this.$refs.asyncapi;
    AsyncApiStandalone.render({ schema, config }, container);
  }
}
</script>

<style scope src="@/assets/asyncapi.min.css"></style>

As you can see on line 6, you need to import AsyncApiStandalone bundle with import AsyncApiStandalone from '@asyncapi/react-component/browser/standalone'; command.

3️⃣ There is one more thing to do. If you like AsyncAPI styling then you need to go to 👉 node_modules/@asyncapi/react-component/style/default.min.css.

Copy that file and then paste it into your assets folder.
I renamed mine asyncapi.min.css.

You can import this in your main.js file with import './assets/asyncapi.min.css' or, as I did, add it at the end of App.vue file with <style scope src="@/assets/asyncapi.min.css"></style>.

Web Components

To generate documentation from your AsyncAPI file, you can use it as an element of an HTML webpage or as a web component in any other web framework you choose. You can do this by implementing web-react-components.

1️⃣ Just create a .html file then copy and paste this code 👇

<script src="https://unpkg.com/@asyncapi/web-component@1.0.0-next.39/lib/asyncapi-web-component.js" defer></script>

<asyncapi-component
  schema='{"asyncapi":"2.4.0","info":{"title":"Account Service","version":"1.0.0","description":"This service is in charge of processing user signups"},"channels":{"user/signedup":{"subscribe":{"message":{"$ref":"#/components/messages/UserSignedUp"}}}},"components":{"messages":{"UserSignedUp":{"payload":{"type":"object","properties":{"displayName":{"type":"string","description":"Name of the user"},"email":{"type":"string","format":"email","description":"Email of the user"}}}}}}}'

  config='{"show": {"sidebar": false}}'

  cssImportPath="https://unpkg.com/@asyncapi/react-component@1.0.0-next.39/styles/default.min.css">
</asyncapi-component>

2️⃣ If you need support for old browsers then you need to add this script as well <script src="https://unpkg.com/@webcomponents/webcomponentsjs@2.5.0/webcomponents-bundle.js"></script>.

That is it! 🤯 Just awesome!

Standalone Bundle

If you want to render documentation from your AsyncAPI file without the use of any framework but with just an HTML webpage then you will need the Standalone bundle.

1️⃣ All you need is just a basic HTML template.

2️⃣ In the head element enter <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://unpkg.com/@asyncapi/react-component@1.0.0-next.39/styles/default.min.css"> to get AsyncAPI document styling.

3️⃣ In the body element type in:

 <div id="asyncapi"></div>

    <script src="https://unpkg.com/@asyncapi/react-component@1.0.0-next.39/browser/standalone/index.js"></script>
    <script>
        AsyncApiStandalone.render({
            schema: '{"asyncapi":"2.4.0","info":{"title":"Account Service","version":"1.0.0","description":"This service is in charge of processing user signups"},"channels":{"user/signedup":{"subscribe":{"message":{"$ref":"#/components/messages/UserSignedUp"}}}},"components":{"messages":{"UserSignedUp":{"payload":{"type":"object","properties":{"displayName":{"type":"string","description":"Name of the user"},"email":{"type":"string","format":"email","description":"Email of the user"}}}}}}}'
            ,
            config: {
                show: {
                    sidebar: false,
                }
            },
        }, document.getElementById('asyncapi'));
    </script>

<script src="https://unpkg.com/@asyncapi/react-component@1.0.0-next.39/browser/standalone/index.js"></script> fetches everything required from the bundle.

I almost forgot.

There is one more way to configure the AsyncAPI component.
You can do it through config props, this same as for normal React component.

My Web Component and Standalone Bundle usage examples have config='{"show": {"sidebar": false}} which turns a sidebar off, but if you change it to true then your rendered document will have a sidebar.

This was only a sample of what could be done. AsyncAPI can do a lot more. Check AsyncAPI documentation for more information on how to generate docs.

I hope that you liked this quick guide through AsyncAPI Client-Side documents generation.

As usual, if you find any mistakes, don't go mad in the comments section. Just let me know and together we can fix it so this article can be even better.
item.author: m1ner

item.title: 程序员的快速眼科提示 | Quick Eye Tips for Programmers
item.description:
由jk开发

发表于整天盯着电脑会让你暴露在有害的蓝光下。 您的眼睛无法阻挡它并导致眼睛疲劳。

必须照顾好你的眼睛，不要拉伤它们。

可以按照 20–20–20 规则来完成——
每 20 分钟休息 20 秒，观察 20 英尺外的物体，

此外，您每分钟可以眨眼 15-20 次。 您还可以使用一个
电脑玻璃可以阻挡来自数字屏幕的蓝光。

作为一名程序员，你自然会暴露在有害的紫外线蓝光下，从一开始就照顾好你的眼睛很重要。

developedbyjk

Staring at the computer all day exposes you to harmful blue light. Your eyes are poor in blocking it out and lead to eye strain.

It is essential to take care of your eyes and not strain them.

It can be done by following 20–20–20 rule-
take a 20-second break every 20 minutes and look at an object 20 feet away,

Moreover you can blink about 15–20 times a minute. You can also use a
computer glass which blocks the blue rays from digital screens.

As a programmer, you are naturally exposed to the harmful UV blue light, and it is important to take care of your eyes from the beginning.

item.title: 训练营的生活！ | Life as a Bootcamper!
item.description:
我清楚地记得我第一次参加我的第一次训练营。 这是一个户外团体健身训练营，在一个炎热而宁静的夏日（虽然不像最近的热浪那么热），在一个美丽宁静的公园里。

我不太确定我在期待什么，但是在 1 小时的课程开始 20 分钟后，我的肺在我的喉咙里，我的心率已经开始了外太空探索！ 我呼吸急促，我以为我会倒在那里，然后需要去医院！ 但你知道吗？ 我很享受！

当健身教练团队无情地带领我们 20 到 30 人完成我们的步伐时，我和他们开玩笑说他们是现实生活中的训练中士，与军事训练营中的那些没有什么不同——当然，这些团体的名字来源于！

迎接挑战，我一周又一周地回来，当我把自己推到我们友好的教练的树皮下时，发生了一些惊人的事情！ 我能够跑得更快更长时间，并将我身体的身体能力推向前所未有的极端。 当然，新撕裂的肌肉的每一根纤维都在向我尖叫，但我正在被磨练成一个更瘦、更强壮、更快、更健康、更有活力的自己——我喜欢它。 我的步态更高了，我的动作更敏捷、轻松和精确，我的头脑也更敏锐了！

结识来自各行各业的具有相似和不同健身水平的新朋友，并一起迎接这些挑战，我学到了很多东西。 我从其他人和他们的个人故事中学习。 之后我们经常去吃早午餐，并就那天我们共同实现的健身挑战和目标、我们正在适应的其他生活方式改变以及见证我们个人韧性的增长而建立联系。 一旦你遇到并完成了一项挑战，你就会变得非常渴望接受更多挑战。

解决训练营的巡回挑战很有趣，看看我可以通过练习做什么，学习我以前从未听说过的新练习，我敢肯定我的腿希望我从来没有做过——比如看似无穷无尽的团队接力圈快节奏的青蛙爬行——看着我成为更好的自己。

技术训练营在这方面非常相似。 就像每周 1 小时拥挤的健身训练营一样。 你在很短的时间内学到了很多东西，而且它会变得非常紧张，特别是如果你像我一样也全职工作并且无论如何生活事件都会继续发生。

然而，你接触了这么多，你的思想和能力也因此而变得敏锐。 你通过了你的第一个专业技术认证，就像我已经完成了我的 AZ-900 Microsoft Fundamentals。 您会惊讶于在如此短的时间内保留了多少新知识。 现在你可以理解和解释几个以前是个谜的技术过程。 例如如何开发、部署和实施各种 Azure 存储、安全和计算解决方案，以及确定哪种解决方案合适以及何时合适。 如何监控、排除故障和优化云解决方案，以及哪些 Azure 工具适用于不同的场景。 如何连接和使用 Azure 和第三方服务。 了解 Azure 的关键概念、架构、管理和治理，以及 Azure 几乎无限的功能和服务。 你变得热情、敏锐并准备好迎接下一个挑战！ 在我的情况下，它将是 AZ-204 Microsoft Developer Associate，然后是 Python 编程语言！

您将结识一些非常了不起的人并与他们建立联系，他们将帮助您塑造和振兴您的旅程。 在塑造和磨练它的同时，您将能够设想出一种您以前从未设想过的生活、社区和版本。 一个更加敏捷、专注于激光的你，拥有更高的能力、技术知识以及学习和做更多事情的渴望和能力——不断改进，同时迎接一个又一个挑战，享受每一步！



原文:

I remember vividly the first time I attended my first ever bootcamp. It was an outdoor group fitness bootcamp in a beautiful serene park on a hot and still summers day (although not as hot as recent heatwaves).

I am not quite sure what I was expecting, but 20 minutes into the 1 hour session and my lungs were in my throat and my heart rate had taken up outerspace exploration! I was breathing so hard, I thought I’d collapse there and then and be in need of a hospital! But you know something? I was enjoying it!

As the team of fitness instructors mercilessly took a group of 20–30 of us through our paces, I joked with them about being real life drill sergeants, not disimilar to the ones found in military bootcamps — where of course, such groups derive their name!

Rising to the challenge, I returned week after week and as I pushed myself under the bark of our friendly instructors, something amazing happened! I was able to run faster for longer, and push the physical capabilities of my body to extremes it had never seen before. Sure every fibre of newly torn muscle screamed expletives at me, but I was being sharpened into a leaner, stronger, faster, fitter and ever more dynamic version of myself — and I loved it. My gait was taller, my movements more agile, effortless and precise and my mind also sharper!

Meeting new people of similar and varying fitness levels and from all walks of life, and rising to those challenges together, I learnt so much. I learnt from others and their personal stories. We would often go to brunch afterward and bond over the fitness challenges and targets we’d achieved together that day, other lifestyle changes we were adapting, and witnessing our growth in personal resilience. Once you’ve met and achieved one challenge you become pretty keen to take on many more.

It was fun to tackle circuit challenges of the bootcamp and see what I could do with practice, learn new exercises I’d never heard of before and I’m sure my legs wish I never did — such as seemingly endless, team relay laps of a fast paced frog-crawl — and watch as I became a better version of myself for it.

Tech bootcamps are very similar in that respect. Much like the weekly 1 hour jam-packed fitness bootcamps. You learn so much in a short space of time and it can get pretty intense, especially if like me you are also working full time and life events continue to happen regardless.

However, you are exposed to so much and your mind and capabilities are sharpened because of it. You pass your first professional tech certification, just as I have achieved my AZ-900 Microsoft Fundamentals. You become amazed by how much new knowledge you have retained in such a short space of time. Now you can understand and explain several tech processes which were previously a mystery. Such as how to develop, deploy and implement a variety of Azure storage, security and compute solutions, and identify which solution is appropriate and when. How to monitor, troubleshoot and optimise cloud solutions and which Azure tools are suitable for different scenarios. How to connect to and consume both Azure and third party services. Knowledge of Azure key concepts, architecture, management and governance, and Azures almost limitless capabilities and services. You become enthused, sharpened and primed for the next challenge! Which in my case will be AZ-204 Microsoft Developer Associate and then programming language Python!

AZ-900 Badge

You’ll meet and network with some pretty amazing people who will help shape and invigorate your journey. You’ll be able to envision a life, community and version of you never conceived before, all while shaping and honing it. A more agile, laser-focused you with increased capabilities, technical know-how and the thirst and capacity to learn and do more — continuously improving, while rising to one challenge after the next and enjoying every step of the way!
item.author: marshac713

item.title: 如果你看到热浪......你会挥手吗？ | If You Saw A Heatwave… Would You Wave Back?
item.description:
如果说英国因一件事而闻名，那就是它经常喜怒无常的天气。 这通常介于寒冷、潮湿和灰色之间，或者明亮，几乎温暖，偶尔会有阵雨。 然而，上周伦敦的气温飙升至炙手可热的 40.2ºC（104.36ºF）。 当您考虑到 7 月的伦敦平均温度通常在 22ºC (71.6ºF) 左右时，那么热浪一词是轻描淡写的。

英国并不是唯一一个气温急剧上升到前所未有的水平的地区。 事实上，几乎整个北半球，包括欧洲、中国、北非和美国的许多其他地区，都屈服于极端高温。 这助长了世界各地的灾难性事件。 由于停机坪融化，一些英国机场被迫关闭，医院计算机系统崩溃，伦敦、法国和希腊各地发生火灾，许多人不得不逃离家园。 仅在一天之内，葡萄牙和西班牙就报告了超过 1000 例与高温有关的死亡（来源：世界经济论坛，2022 年 7 月 22 日）。

随着温度持续上升，预计这种全球热浪将成为一种更频繁的常态，人们不再否认气候变化实际上是非常真实的，可能是残酷的致命并且正在这里发生，现在。 几十年来，著名科学家和臭名昭著的气候活动家发出的狂热警告，在喜剧《别抬头》中被讽刺的冷漠或否认。 可以清楚地看到一颗威胁灭绝级别事件的彗星在天空中飞向地球，但全球的反应并没有试图阻止它，而是简单地“不要抬头”。 因为抬头就意味着采取行动。 采取行动将意味着巨额的金钱投资，并对我们已经开始享受的经常挥霍的生活方式做出不受欢迎的改变。

然而，人们对大众的态度终于开始发生变化。 全球变暖不再是我们曾曾孙辈眼中的事情，正如我们在过去几天所见证的那样，它现在正在发生。 过去 7 年是有记录以来最热的 7 年。

因此，政府和企业越来越多地转向可持续技术来拯救我们的世界。 这不仅带来了希望，而且带来了许多令我敬畏的迷人创新。

例如，苏黎世联邦理工学院的瑞士科学家开发了一种技术，可以利用阳光和空气制造喷气燃料！ 该过程产生了一种可再生和碳中性形式的煤油，燃烧时只会将二氧化碳释放回空气中，而这些二氧化碳最初是从空气中提取出来的。 所以这实际上是凭空制造燃料！

葡萄牙建造了一个巨大的浮动太阳能公园，有 4 个足球场那么大，包含 12,000 块太阳能电池板。 太阳能公园停泊在葡萄牙阿尔克瓦水库上，该水库恰好是西欧最大的人工湖。 太阳能公园每年将产生 7.5 吉瓦时的电力，足以为 1,500 户家庭供电，费用仅为燃气发电厂的三分之一。 包括中国和德国在内的世界各地都建造了类似的水上公园，以减少对破坏环境的化石燃料的依赖。

无人机技术被用于扩大重新造林工作。 由于气候变化引起的丛林大火，大量森林消失。 随着地球这些重要肺的丧失，还有支持我们生态系统的美丽生物多样性。 无人机能够以人类无法实现的规模、速度、准确性和效率重新种植森林，有助于扭转全球变暖造成的大部分破坏。 由于时间是这里的关键因素，以这种方式使用的技术是惊人的：

看似直接来自科幻小说的 ELSA-d，是一颗配备强大磁铁的微型卫星，用于清理太空污染：

麻省理工学院的科学家雄心勃勃地希望彻底扭转气候变化。 机器人将被用来制造巨大的气泡，然后将它们聚集在一起形成一个巴西大小的木筏，然后部署到太空中。 这个木筏将形成一个盾牌，将大量的太阳热量从地球反射出去：

这种创造性的创新正是我对技术的热爱。 可能性几乎是无限的，当用于善意时，可以对现实世界产生巨大的影响，保护我们所知道的生命。



原文:

If there’s one thing the UK is famed for it is its often temperamental weather. This usually settles somewhere on the spectrum between cold, damp and grey, or bright, and almost warm with sporadic showers. Yet last week saw temperatures skyrocket in London to a sizzling 40.2ºC(104.36ºF). When you consider that the average London temparature for July is usually around 22ºC (71.6ºF), then the term heatwave is an understatement.

The UK was not the only region to see temperatures rise to unprecedented levels so dramatically. In fact almost the entire northern hemisphere, including many other parts of Europe, China, North Africa and the United States succumbed to extreme heat. This fueled catastrophic events all around the world. Some UK airports were forced to close due to melting tarmac, hospital computer systems crashed, fires broke out all over London, France and Greece, with many having to flee their homes. On one day alone, Portugal and Spain reported in excess of 1000 heat-related deaths (Source:World Economic Forum 22 Jul 2022).

With such global heatwaves predicted to become a more frequent norm, as temperatures continue to rise, there is nolonger any denying that climate change is in fact very real, can be cruelly fatal and is happening right here, right now. For decades, feverish warnings from eminent scientist and infamous climate activists, have been met with the type of nonchalance or denial satirised in the comedy, Dont Look Up. In which a comet threatening an extinction level event, can clearly be seen in the sky, hurtling towards Earth, yet instead of trying to stop it, the global response is simply to “Dont Look Up”. For looking up would mean taking action. Taking action would mean huge monetary investment and making unpopular changes to the often profligate lifestyles we have come to enjoy.

However, attitudes are finally beginning to change on mass. Global warming is no longer something on our great great great grandchildrens horizons, as we have witnessed over the last few days, it is happening now. The past 7 years have been the hottest since records began.

As such, governments and corporations are increasingly turning to sustainable technology to literally save our world. This has borne not only hope, but many fascinating innovations which have me in awe.

For example Swiss Scientists at ETH Zurich have developed a technology which can make jet fuel from sunlight and air! The process creates a renewable and carbon-neutral form of kerosene which when burned would only release Co2 back into the air which was extracted from it in the first instance. So thats literally making fuel out of thin air!

Portugal has built an enormous floating solar park, the size of 4 football pitches, containing 12,000 solar panels. The solar park is moored on Portugals Alqueva reservoir, which happens to be Western Europes largest artificial lake,. The solar park will generate 7.5 gigawatt hours of electricity each year, which is enough to power 1,500 homes, at one third of the expense of a gas-fired plant. Similar floating parks have ben built all over the world, including China and Germany, in a bid to reduce reliance on environmentally destructive, fossil fuels.

Drone technology is being used to scale reforestation efforts. A devastating number of forests are lost to bush fires caused by climate change. Along with the loss of these vital lungs of the Earth is the beautiful biodiversity which supports our ecosystem. Drones are able to replant forests at a scale, speed, accuracy, and efficiency which cannot be achieved by human effort alone, helping to reverse much of the damage caused by global warming. As time is the crucial factor here, technology being used in this way is phenomenal:

Seemingly straight out of a science fiction novel, ELSA-d, a miniture satellite equipped with a powerful magnet is being used to clean-up pollution from space:

MIT scientist are ambitiously hoping to completely reverse climate change. Robots will be used to create giant bubbles which are then clumped together to form a raft the size of Brazil, then deployed into space. This raft will form a shield which reflects a significant amount of the suns heat away from Earth:

This creative innovation is exactly what I love about technology. The possibilities are almost limitless and when used for good can have a tremendous real world impact, preserving life as we know it.

item.title: 关于我的一点... | A Bit About Me…
item.description:
亲爱的读者您好！ 欢迎到我的博客！

关于我的一点：我是一名软件工程师实习生，目前正在学习 Microsoft Azure 云计算和 Python。 我还精通 JavaScript、HTML 和 CSS，因此我正在转换职业、转向和快速进入科技世界！ 这是一个令人兴奋的地方！ 想想詹姆斯韦伯望远镜和它能够从太空捕捉到的令人叹为观止的美丽照片。 有这么多星系，毕竟那里可能有生命！ 这就是我喜欢科技的地方——如果你能想到它，你最终就可以创造它！ 我们现在实际上生活在科幻电影所设想的未来！ 这就是跨学科合作和思想交流的伟大之处！ 如此多伟大的创新从我们的想象中解救出来，并以尖叫的方式栩栩如生！！！

很高兴见到你！ 欢迎来到我的博客——欢迎来到我的世界！ 您也可以在这里与我联系： linktr.ee/marshacastello

Hello Dear Reader! Welcome to my Blog!

A bit about me: I am a Software Engineer Trainee currently learning Microsoft Azure Cloud Computing and Python. I am also knowledgeable of JavaScript, HTML and CSS, as such I am career switching, pivoting and piroueeting into the world of Tech! 🩰

Its an exciting place to be! Think James Webb telescope and the beautiful breath arresting photos it was able to capture from space. With that many galaxies, there just may be life out there after all! 👽 😉 Think the metaverse where people will be able to create their own worlds, universes and multiverses (yes I’m a Marvel fan! How could you not be?! 🎞 ) just like they did on the holideck in Star-Trek!!!

Thats what I love about tech — if you can conceive it you can eventually create it! We are now practically living in the future envisioned by sci-fi movies! Thats the great thing about interdisciplinary collaboration and the cross fertilisation of ideas! So many great innovations are rescued from the confines of our imaginations and brought screamingly to life !!!

Pleased to meet you! Welcome to my blog — welcome to my world! You can also connect with me here: linktr.ee/marshacastello

item.title: 单例模式 trong Javascript | Singleton Pattern trong Javascript
item.description:
完美的！ - - -完毕 - - -
对象 được tạo đó có 值 là 实例 của 类。

Tuy nhiên, việc implementation một class ở các ví dụ trên thật sự CHỈ ĐỂ HỌC CHO BIẾT THÔI CHỨ MÉO GIÚP ÍCH ĐƯỢC . Tuy nhiên, việc implementation một class ở các ví dụ trên thật sự CHỈ ĐỂ HỌC CHO BIẾT THÔI CHỨ MÉO GIÚP ÍCH ĐƯỢC 。 Vì ngày nay ở một app ReactJS/NextJS, chúng ta thường sử dụng các global state thông qua các hệ sinh thái như Redux hoặc Context thay vì sử dụng Singletons. Vì ngày nay ở một app ReactJS/NextJS, chúng ta thường sử dụng các global state thông qua các hệ sinh thái như Redux hoặc Context thay vì sử dụng Singletons。 Mặc dù global state của chúng có vẻ tương tự như Singleton, nhưng chúng chỉ cho read-only state thay vì thay đổi trực tiếp state của Singleton. Mặc dù global state của chúng có vẻ tương tự như Singleton，nhưng chúng chỉ cho只读状态thay vì thay đổi trực tiếp state của Singleton。 Khi sử dụng Redux/Context chỉ có function reducers có thể update các state, sau khi một component đã gửi một action thông qua dispatcher. Khi sử dụng Redux/Context chỉ có function reducers có thể update các state, sau khi một component đã gửi một action thông qua dispatcher。


Tóm cái quần lại , thay vì sử dụng Singleton để quản lý một app thì suy nghĩ nên dẹp đi, vì giờ đây đã có Redux/ Context. Tóm cái quần lại , thay vì sử dụng Singleton để quản lý một app thì suy nghĩ nên dẹp đi, vì giờ đây đã có Redux/ Context。


原文:

Singletons được hiểu là các class có thể được khởi tạo một lần và có thể truy cập trên toàn cục (global). Các class này có thể chia sẽ các state trong toàn bộ app, thì Singletons là một thứ tuyệt vời để quản lý global state trong một app.
Ví dụ tôi sẽ xây dựng một class Counter có:

  • a getInstance là phương thức trả về value của của instance
  • a getCount trả về value hiện tại của biến counter
  • an increment method làm tăng value của counter lên 1
  • a decrement method làm giảm value của counter xuống 1 
let counter = 0;

class Counter {
  getInstance() {
    return this;
  }

  getCount() {
    return counter;
  }

  increment() {
    return ++counter;
  }

  decrement() {
    return --counter;
  }
}

Tuy nhiên class này không đáp ứng các yêu cầu cho một Singleton! Một Singleton chỉ có thể được khởi tạo một lần. Hiện tại, chúng ta có thể tạo nhiều phiên bản của class Counter

let counter = 0;

class Counter {
  getInstance() {
    return this;
  }

  getCount() {
    return counter;
  }

  increment() {
    return ++counter;
  }

  decrement() {
    return --counter;
  }
}

const counter1 = new Counter();
const counter2 = new Counter();

console.log(counter1.getInstance() === counter2.getInstance()); // false

Bằng cách gọi method new 2 lần, tôi đặt obj tên counter1 và counter2. Value được trả về bởi method getInstance của counter1 và counter2, nhưng chúng trả về các tham chiếu: chúng không hoàn toàn bằng nhau!

Hãy đảm bảo rằng class Counter chỉ có thể tạo một lần
Để đảm bảo rằng một instance có thể được tạo, đó là tạo một biến variable được gọi là instance. Trong constructor của class Counter, tôi có thể set instance bằng một tham chiếu đến instance khi một instance mới được tạo.

let instance;
let counter = 0;

class Counter {
  constructor() {
    if (instance) {
      throw new Error("You can only create one instance!");
    }
    instance = this;
  }

  getInstance() {
    return this;
  }

  getCount() {
    return counter;
  }

  increment() {
    return ++counter;
  }

  decrement() {
    return --counter;
  }
}

const counter1 = new Counter();
const counter2 = new Counter();
// Error: You can only create one instance!

Perfect! Bây giờ tôi không thể tạo nhiều multiple cho instance hơn nữa.

Hãy export Counter từ file counter.js. Nhưng trước khi làm điều đó, tôi nên đảm bảo rằng code không thể sửa đổi Singleton. Không thể thêm hoặc sửa các property của một đối tượng, điều này làm giảm rủi ro vô tình overwriting các value trên Singleton.

let instance;
let counter = 0;

class Counter {
  constructor() {
    if (instance) {
      throw new Error("You can only create one instance!");
    }
    instance = this;
  }

  getInstance() {
    return this;
  }

  getCount() {
    return counter;
  }

  increment() {
    return ++counter;
  }

  decrement() {
    return --counter;
  }
}

const singletonCounter = Object.freeze(new Counter());
export default singletonCounter;

-----DONE-----
Nhược điểm
Việc khởi tạo trong một instance có thể tiết kiệm rất nhiều memory. Thay vì phải tạo memory cho từng instance mỗi lần new, tôi chỉ cần tạo 1 memory cho 1 instance đó, và memory này được referenced cho toàn bộ app. Tuy nhiên Singleton được coi là một anti-pattern, nên thường không được sử dụng trong Javascript. Trong nhiều programming languages, như java, c++, c#, không thể trực tiếp tạo các object theo cách tôi có thể làm trong javascript. Trong OOP, chúng ta cần tạo một class, class này tạo ra object. Object được tạo đó có value là instance của class.

Tuy nhiên, việc implementation một class ở các ví dụ trên thật sự CHỈ ĐỂ HỌC CHO BIẾT THÔI CHỨ MÉO GIÚP ÍCH ĐƯỢC. Vì ngày nay ở một app ReactJS/NextJS, chúng ta thường sử dụng các global state thông qua các hệ sinh thái như Redux hoặc Context thay vì sử dụng Singletons. Mặc dù global state của chúng có vẻ tương tự như Singleton, nhưng chúng chỉ cho read-only state thay vì thay đổi trực tiếp state của Singleton. Khi sử dụng Redux/Context chỉ có function reducers có thể update các state, sau khi một component đã gửi một action thông qua dispatcher.


Tóm cái quần lại, thay vì sử dụng Singleton để quản lý một app thì suy nghĩ nên dẹp đi, vì giờ đây đã có Redux/ Context.
item.author: dylanvo28

item.title: 如何在子 shell 中运行命令并在与 Bash/ZSH 兼容的后台抑制输出 | How to run commands in a sub-shell and suppress output in the background that's Bash/ZSH compatible
item.description:
奥布里波特伍德

发表于因此，基本上，它的作用是运行一个子 shell，然后将您的命令隐藏在另一个发送到后台的子 shell 中。 第一个子 shell 基本上捕获来自发送到后台的第二个子 shell 的输出。

因为我在我的 zsh 脚本中使用了 shellcheck ，所以下面在 shellcheck 中标记了一个未知错误（因为 shellcheck 目前不支持 zsh ）并且它不会对文件进行 lint：

 () ( foo bar ) &> /dev/null &!

具体来说是&! 这不起作用（但它是抑制后台输出的有效 ZSH 指令）。 我至少会使用大多数建议，例如在 Stack Exchange 上获得工作输出。 所以，我在没有答案的情况下进入了谷歌搜索兔子洞，直到我登陆：

然后我不得不摸索可怕的格式，并想出了上面与 Bash 兼容的内容，可以在后台运行命令而没有输出！

马科斯·恩里克—— 阿里绿心—— 一旦暂停，aubreypwd 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，aubreypwd 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，aubreypwd 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 aubreypwd 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 aubreypwd 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 aubreypwd 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Aubrey Portwood

Posted on Jul 30

TLDR: This works:

So, basically, what this does is run a sub-shell then bury your commands in another sub-shell that is sent to the background. The first sub-shell basically catches the output from the second sub-shell which was send to the background.

&!

Because I use shellcheck with my zsh scripts the following flagged an unknown error in shellcheck (because shellcheck does not support zsh at the moment) and it wouldn't lint the file:

() ( foo bar ) &> /dev/null &!

Specifically it was the &! that wasn't working (but is a valid ZSH directive to suppress background output). I would always get job output at the least using most of the suggestions e.g. on Stack Exchange. So, I went down a Googling-rabbit-hole with no answers, until I landed on:

→ https://www.baeldung.com/linux/run-multiple-commands-in-background

Which I then had to grok the horrible formatting and came up with the above that is Bash compatible to run commands in the background with no output!

🙌

Marcos Henrique - Jun 22 '21

Ali GreenHeart - Jun 18 '21

Deepa Subramanian - Jun 15 '21

Pratik Kumar Panda - Jun 12 '21
